title: invariant-at-stable
manager: `invariant` — a condition that must hold at every stable point of the machine, checked after each macrostep settles. Invariants are the machine-wide truths ("the queue is never negative," "exactly one connection is active") that no transition may violate, and checking them at stable points is what makes them a property of the whole machine rather than of any single edge. The third pillar of design-by-contract, and the bridge to the proofs era.
cs: `invariant <expr>` checked at each stable point (§11, fsl#1355); after the macrostep commits (W6.71); violation per W6.65; feeds era-3 verification.
